docs.title: Install dependencies
docs.text:
sudo apt update && \
  sudo apt install python3-pip git git-lfs curl tar unzip bzip2 ffmpeg

Info

The application uses sudo, if you don't have it installed by default, install it.

docs.title: Install application
docs.text:
• Method 1 - pip
  • Method 2 - pyz
  • Method 3 - source

docs.title: Install Aurora CLI from PyPi
docs.text:
The standard way to distribute python packages.

python3 -m pip install aurora-cli

docs.title: Install Aurora CLI *.pyz
docs.text: This method is as simple as possible - the entire application is in a pyz file.

docs.title: Create folder
docs.text: mkdir ~/.local/opt

docs.title: Download
docs.text:
wget -x https://github.com/keygenqt/aurora-cli/raw/main/builds/aurora-cli-2.4.7.pyz \
  -O ~/.local/opt/aurora-cli.pyz

docs.title: Add alias to ~/.bashrc
docs.text: alias aurora-cli='python3 ~/.local/opt/aurora-cli.pyz'

docs.title: Update environment
docs.text: source ~/.bashrc

docs.title: Install Aurora CLI from GitHub
docs.text: This method is suitable for development.

docs.title: Create folder
docs.text: mkdir -p ~/.local/opt/aurora-cli

docs.title: Clone project
docs.text: git clone https://github.com/keygenqt/aurora-cli.git ~/.local/opt/aurora-cli

docs.title: Open folder project
docs.text: cd ~/.local/opt/aurora-cli

docs.title: Init environment
docs.text: virtualenv .venv

docs.title: Open environment
docs.text: source .venv/bin/activate

docs.title: Install requirements
docs.text: pip install -r requirements.txt

docs.title: Run app
docs.text: python -m aurora_cli

docs.title: Using Aurora CLI
docs.text:
The application allows you to simplify routine work with the Aurora OS toolchain, simplify lengthy commands and combine disparate Linux functionality in one simple application.

For example, you want to validate a package:

  1. You need to get the name of the target.
  2. You need to enter the command.

This is what we roughly get in the standard version:

aurora_psdk sb2 -t "AuroraOS-5.0.0.60-base-aarch64" -m emulate rpm-validator {path}

Or an option with Aurora CLI:

aurora-cli psdk validate -p {path}

And your target will be specified by the application. This includes installing the package on the device and the emulator, and installing: Platfrom SDK, Aurora SDK Flutter SDK, package signing and much more.

It's easier with Aurora CLI!

docs.title: Aurora CLI - Configuration
docs.text:
When you first launch the application, you will be prompted to create a configuration file. You can find it along the path: ~/.aurora-cli/configuration.yaml. You can adjust it to suit yourself by adding keys or devices.

It is described in sufficient detail, I present it here:

## Application configuration file Aurora CLI
## Version config: 0.0.3

## The parameter sets the path to install psdk and search sdk
## Specify an existing directory
workdir: ~/

## Type output: short | command | verbose
output: short

## Path to sign keys
## name - The name you will see in the list
## key  - Path to the key.pem file
## cert - Path to the cert.pem file
keys:
  - name: Public
    key: ~/.aurora-cli/keys/regular_key.pem
    cert: ~/.aurora-cli/keys/regular_cert.pem

## Devices list
## ip       - Device IP WI-FI or cable connection
## pass     - SSH password
## port     - SSH port
## devel-su - Device root password
devices:
  - ip: 192.168.2.15
    pass: '00000'
    port: 22
    devel-su: '00000'

Info

The application will also offer you to download public keys.

docs.title: Available
docs.text:
Get available devices from configuration.

Example

aurora-cli device available

docs.title: Command
docs.text:
Execute the command on the device.

Params

  • --execute (-e) - Command to execute on the device.
  • --index (-i) - Device index from the config.
  • --verbose (-v) - Detailed output.

Example

aurora-cli device command -e 'ls'

docs.title: Run
docs.text:
Run package on device in container.

Params

  • --package (-p) - Package name app for run in container.
  • --index (-i) - Device index from the config.
  • --verbose (-v) - Detailed output.

Example

aurora-cli device run -p {package name}

docs.title: Install
docs.text:
Install RPM package on device.

Params

  • --path (-p) - Path to RPM package.
  • --index (-i) - Device index from the config.
  • --verbose (-v) - Detailed output.

Example

aurora-cli device install -p {path}.rpm

docs.title: Upload
docs.text:
Upload file to ~/Download directory device.

Params

  • --path (-p) - Path to file.
  • --index (-i) - Device index from the config.

Example

aurora-cli device upload -p {path}

docs.title: Start
docs.text:
Start emulator.

Params

  • --verbose (-v) - Detailed output.

Example

aurora-cli emulator start

docs.title: Screenshot
docs.text:
Take screenshot emulator.

Params

  • --verbose (-v) - Detailed output.

Example

aurora-cli emulator screenshot

docs.title: Recording
docs.text:
Recording video from emulator.

Params

  • --convert (-c) - Convert video to mp4.
  • --verbose (-v) - Detailed output.

Example

aurora-cli emulator recording

docs.title: Command
docs.text:
Execute the command on the emulator.

Params

  • --execute (-e) - Command to execute on the emulator.
  • --verbose (-v) - Detailed output.

Example

aurora-cli emulator command -e 'ls'

docs.title: Run
docs.text:
Run package on emulator in container.

Params

  • --package (-p) - Package name app for run in container.
  • --verbose (-v) - Detailed output.

Example

aurora-cli emulator run -p {package name}

docs.title: Install
docs.text:
Install RPM package on emulator.

Params

  • --path (-p) - Path to RPM package.
  • --verbose (-v) - Detailed output.

Example

aurora-cli emulator install -p {path}.rpm

docs.title: Upload
docs.text:
Upload file to ~/Download directory emulator.

Params

  • --path (-p) - Path to file.

Example

aurora-cli emulator upload -p {path}

docs.title: Aurora CLI - Flutter for Aurora OS
Available
docs.text:
Get available versions flutter.

Example

aurora-cli flutter available

docs.title: Install
docs.text:
Install Flutter SDK for Aurora OS.

Params

  • --latest (-l) - Aurora select latest version for install.

Example

aurora-cli flutter install --latest

docs.title: Installed
docs.text:
Get installed list Flutter SDK.

Example

aurora-cli flutter installed

docs.title: Remove
docs.text:
Remove Flutter SDK.

Example

aurora-cli flutter remove

docs.title: Build
docs.text:
Add script to project for build Flutter application.

Params

  • --index (-i) - Specify index version.
  • --yes (-y) - All yes confirm.

Example

aurora-cli flutter build

docs.title: Debug gdb
docs.text:
Project configure and run for gdb debug.

Params

  • --index (-i) - Specify index device.
  • --verbose (-v) - Detailed output.

Example

aurora-cli flutter debug gdb

docs.title: Debug dart
docs.text:
Project configure and run on device for dart debug or hot reload.

Params

  • --index (-i) - Specify index device.
  • --yes (-y) - All yes confirm.
  • --verbose (-v) - Detailed output.

Example

aurora-cli flutter debug dart

docs.title: Plugins
docs.text:
Get types plugins info. Run in folder with pubspec.yaml.

Example

aurora-cli flutter plugins

docs.title: Icons
docs.text:
Create icons size for flutter project.

Params

  • --path (-p) - Path to image.

Example

aurora-cli flutter icons -p {/path/to/file.png}

docs.title: Available
docs.text:
Get available version Aurora Platform SDK.

Example

aurora-cli psdk available

docs.title: Install
docs.text:
Download and install Aurora Platform SDK.

Params

  • --latest (-l) - Aurora select latest version for install.

Example

aurora-cli psdk install --latest

docs.title: Installed
docs.text:
Get installed list Aurora Platform SDK.

Example

aurora-cli psdk installed

docs.title: Remove
docs.text:
Remove installed Aurora Platform SDK.

Example

aurora-cli psdk remove

docs.title: Sudoers
docs.text:
Add/Del sudoers permissions Aurora Platform SDK.

Params

  • --delete (-d) - For delete sudoers permissions.

Example

aurora-cli psdk sudoers

docs.title: List targets
docs.text:
Get list targets.

Example

aurora-cli psdk list-targets

docs.title: Sign
docs.text:
Sign (with re-sign) RPM package.

Params

  • --path (-p) - Path to RPM package.
  • --index (-i) - Keys index from the config.
  • --verbose (-v) - Detailed output.

Example

aurora-cli psdk sign -p {path}

docs.title: Validate
docs.text:
Validate RPM packages.

Params

  • --path (-p) - Path to RPM package.
  • --verbose (-v) - Detailed output.

Example

aurora-cli psdk validate -p {path}

docs.title: Package install
docs.text:
Install RPM packages to target.

Params

  • --path (-p) - Path to RPM package.
  • --verbose (-v) - Detailed output.

Example

aurora-cli psdk package-install -p {path}

docs.title: Package remove
docs.text:
Remove RPM packages from target.

Params

  • --package (-p) - Package name.
  • --verbose (-v) - Detailed output.

Example

aurora-cli psdk package-remove -p {package.name}

docs.title: Package search
docs.text:
Search installed RPM packages in target.

Params

  • --package (-p) - Package name.
  • --verbose (-v) - Detailed output.

Example

aurora-cli psdk package-search -p {package.name}

docs.title: Clear
docs.text:
Remove snapshots targets.

Params

  • --verbose (-v) - Detailed output.

Example

aurora-cli psdk clear

docs.title: Available
docs.text:
Get available version Aurora SDK.

Example

aurora-cli sdk available

docs.title: Install
docs.text:
Download and run install Aurora SDK.

Params

  • --latest (-l) - Aurora select latest version for install.
  • --install-type (-t) - Download installer offline or online (default - online).

Example

aurora-cli sdk install --latest

docs.title: Installed
docs.text:
Get version installed Aurora SDK.

Example

aurora-cli sdk installed

docs.title: Tool
docs.text:
Run maintenance tool (remove, update).

Example

aurora-cli sdk tool
